title: Threat Modeling, SSRF, XML and Content Budgets, Supply Chain, Secrets, and Retention · Transfer Build
summary: An independent university archive receives a different publisher mix, unknown XML shape, validator state, tenant, async schedule, database condition, accessibility need, platform, and injected failure and transfers threat modeling, ssrf, xml and content budgets, supply chain, secrets, and retention into a changed defense-in-depth TypeScript feed control set. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of defense-in-depth TypeScript feed control set with automated and judgme…
objectives: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk through every async data flow.
Combine URL admission, redirect and address checks, proxy controls, egress policy, metadata blocking, DNS rebinding evidence, and operational response.
Align compressed bytes, decoded XML, entity expansion, object depth, normalization, sanitizer, search, pagination, bulk, export, and telemetry limits.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Feed aggregation and TypeScript Commit to the mission model · Lab Feed Ts Threat Dataflow
steps.instruction:
lab case — A lab TypeScript team handles case 7654 during an inclusive cross-device research reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to prove threat controls, SSRF and amplification budgets, package and secret policy, retention, and erasure; the release claim must bind source, compiler, emitted JavaScript, migrations, lockfile, package, image, configuration, and restored state; then inject one URL, DNS, redirect, stream, compression, timeout, abort, or publisher-budget failure and settle ownership. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original RSS, Atom, HTTP, PostgreSQL, and reader fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, fast-xml-parser, Express, PostgreSQL, processes, signals, handle and load claims, containers, accessibility review, backups, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Trace assets, actors, trust boundaries, entry points, abuse cases, controls, detection, response, and residual risk through every async data flow.

What must be established before implementation?
